book_summary: Exodus has chapters 1-31 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The completed material runs from names and oppression through liberation, wilderness formation, Sinai covenant preparation, Ten Words, covenant ordinances, covenant ratification, and tabernacle/priesthood instructions. Key themes include YHWH's seeing/hearing/knowing, liberation from forced service, Pesach/Passover, manna/Sabbath enoughness, Jethro's distributed leadership, covenant identity as priestly kingdom/holy nation, liberation-shaped Torah/instruction, justice for vulnerable people, sacred architecture, priestly representation, Spirit-filled craft, and Sabbath as covenant sign.

translation_notes: Hebrew-first approach required: Shemot/Names, YHWH, Elohim, avad/avodah, berit, ruach, pesach, mishkan, kavod, chesed where relevant.
Avad/avodah is a central thread: Pharaoh turns service into crushing bondage; YHWH liberates for covenant service/worship.
YHWH name disclosure in Exodus 3:14 preserves Hebrew verbal ambiguity: I AM / I WILL BE.
Pharaoh hardening language must track Hebrew verbs carefully: hazaq, kaved, qashah require later lexical audit.
Do not demonize Egyptians ethnically; critique Pharaoh, empire, forced labor, and fear-based domination.
Manna economy emphasizes enoughness: much had no excess and little had no lack.
Sabbath appears as gift before Sinai law code; rest is liberation practice.
Sinai covenant begins with liberation and eagle-wing rescue before command.
Ten Words should be framed as liberation-shaped covenant instruction, not abstract legalism.
Ancient slavery, patriarchy, violence, and property laws in Exodus 21-23 require source preservation plus anti-domination safeguards. They must not be treated as timeless moral ideals.
Sojourner ethics are grounded in knowing the soul/life of the sojourner from Egypt memory.
Land promise and displacement language require later ethical audit and must not be used to justify modern dispossession or violence.
Tabernacle begins with willing-hearted contribution, not coercive extraction.
Mishkan/sanctuary is for YHWH dwelling in the people's midst after liberation.
Ark poles and altar/table poles preserve portable presence with the wilderness people.
Priestly garments symbolize representative burden-bearing, not status theater: names of Israel on shoulders and heart.
Craft is Spirit-filled wisdom: Bezalel, Oholiab, and wise-hearted artisans are theological actors.
Sabbath closes tabernacle instructions: even sacred work stops for covenant rest.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Exodus 3:14 YHWH/Ehyeh asher ehyeh requires deep lexical and reception-history audit.
Exodus 4:21 and hardening motif should receive verb-by-verb audit across Exodus.
Exodus land promise / named peoples requires ethical and historical reception audit before later conquest texts.
Exodus 12 Pesach/Passover terminology and calendar reset require Hebrew ritual/source audit.
Exodus 20 Ten Words require Decalogue tradition comparison with Deuteronomy 5.
Exodus 21 slavery/servant laws require ancient Near Eastern context and anti-slavery reception-history audit.
Exodus 22:18 sorcery prohibition requires dangerous reception-history safeguards.
Exodus 23 land/displacement language requires ethical audit before any application.
Exodus 24 covenant ratification/blood language requires ritual-source audit.
Exodus 25-31 tabernacle design requires ancient cultic/material/source audit.
Exodus 25 kapporet/cover/atonement-cover terminology requires lexical and later reception audit.
Exodus 28 Urim/Thummim requires ancient divination/priestly discernment audit.
Exodus 29 ordination/filling-hand ritual requires priestly source audit.
Exodus 30 census/ransom silver requires anti-wealth-hierarchy audit.
Exodus 31 Sabbath penalty language requires ethical/reception-history audit.
next_actions: Continue Exodus with chapters 32-34: golden calf rupture, Moses intercession, broken tablets, Divine presence, YHWH's character proclamation, renewed covenant, and shining face.
